steps.title: Recall the complementary angle property
steps.content: In a right triangle, the sum of all angles is $180°$. With one right angle ($90°$), the two acute angles must sum to $180° - 90° = 90°$. They are complementary.

steps.title: Calculate the other acute angle
steps.content:
If one acute angle measures $51°$, then the other acute angle is:
$$90° - 51° = 39°$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
